g.text: Surplus produce from local market stands.
A menu built together from what arrives.
Everyone who walks in is a volunteer
Foodbank Maastricht is a shared kitchen,
not a service counter. People come to
chop, stir, wash, laugh, eat, and keep
good food moving through the city instead
Pull up a chair at Landbouwbelang for the
Placeholder snapshots for now, ready for real market hauls,
Bring your hands, your appetite, or your support.
Built for Foodbank Maastricht. Replace placeholder photos with community images when ready.
Foodbank Maastricht was founded over 12 years ago at
Landbouwbelang, a cultural community space in
Maastricht. What began as a practical response to food
waste became a steady weekly ritual.
From market leftovers to Friday dinner
Leave for the Markt to collect leftover fruit and vegetables.
Return to Landbouwbelang, decide the menu, and start cooking.
Sit down together for a vegan three-course dinner.
There is no hard line between organizers, cooks,
guests, and helpers. Everyone who walks in is
Built for Foodbank Maastricht. Replace placeholder photos with community images when ready.
Show up on Friday and become
Come to Landbouwbelang on Friday afternoon to help
collect food, cook, clean, serve, or welcome guests.
